own_review.issue: The one-boolean fix (optional false->true) is safe and correct: the 6-arg MetadataFactory.createRequirement overload keeps greedy=true by default, so resolvable extra.classpath entries are still pulled in greedily while unresolvable ones (platform-specific fragments) no longer fail resolution. Matches PDE behavior and existing repo precedents: InstallableUnitResolver.java:225 (optionalGreedySourceBundleRequirement, args true,false,true) and P2ResolverImpl.java:363 (optionalGreedyRequirementTo, args true,true,true).

No test covers the new behavior: the existing IT only runs resolvable extra-classpath projects with verifyErrorFreeLog; nothing exercises an unresolvable platform: URL entry to guard the fix against regression.

Side effect: typo'd or genuinely missing jars.extra.classpath entries are now silently ignored with no warning (previously a hard resolution error). Acceptable since PDE behaves the same, but a debug/warn log on unsatisfied optional extra-classpath requirements would aid diagnosis.

Scope note: the ICapabilityAdvice from getExtraEntriesAdvice is added as publisher advice, so the optional flag lands in generated p2 metadata for both the global jars.extra.classpath and per-jar extra.<jar> entries.
Import reorder of StatusTool/OptionalResolutionAction is cosmetic noise unrelated to the fix (restores alphabetical order).

points.point: optional is the 5th param; verified against BundleDependenciesAction.java:162-168 explicit /* optional */ comment; change makes platform-specific entries non-fatal
points.note: Citation verified real; correct parameter analysis. But never mentions greedy.

points.point: OptionalResolutionAction is passed to getPublisherActions but not getPublisherAdvice, so optional=true here is hardcoded regardless of configuration
points.note: Verified: AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:56-64 confirms the plumbing. Genuine design observation beyond the human.

points.point: No test for unresolvable-entry scenario; existing ExtraClasspathEntriesTest only covers resolvable entries
points.note: Substance verified (actual file ExtraClassPathEntriesTest.java, minor casing misname; tests indeed only verifyErrorFreeLog on resolvable projects).

points.point: Cosmetic import reorder, no functional impact

missed: Human's key insight: the 6-arg createRequirement overload defaults greedy=true, which is what makes the optional-only flip safe (entries still included when resolvable). v5 shows zero awareness of the greedy dimension.

points.point: Change semantically correct; pattern consistent with InstallableUnitResolver.java:225 and P2ResolverImpl.java:363 optionalGreedyRequirementTo
points.note: Both precedent citations verified real and are exactly the precedents that validate the human's greedy insight; v7 names the optional+greedy pattern though it stops short of explicitly stating the 6-arg overload defaults greedy=true.

points.point: Change affects both jars.extra.classpath and per-jar extra.<jar> entries via extractExtraEntriesAsIURequirement
points.note: Verified: AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:93-97 routes both through the same helper.

points.point: Suggest IT for unresolvable scenario; existing TYCHO253extraClassPathEntries tests only cover resolvable entries
points.note: Cited path exists; claim about test coverage verified true.

points.point: Import reorder cosmetic, maintains alphabetical order

exceeds: Precedent citations (the exact verified optional+greedy precedents)
Missing-test point with correct IT path

qualitative: Tiny 1-line semantic PR where the human's whole review is the greedy-default insight: the 6-arg createRequirement overload keeps greedy=true, so flipping only 'optional' already gives the desired optional-but-greedy behavior. v7 effectively captures this by citing the exact verified optional+greedy precedents (InstallableUnitResolver.java:225, P2ResolverImpl.java:363) and naming the optionalGreedyRequirementTo helper, though it never states the greedy default explicitly; v5 never mentions greediness at all and so misses the reference's core point despite otherwise solid parameter verification. Both reviews are fully grounded — every file, line, and coverage claim checked out, zero absence-fabrications on either side — and both add a legitimate missing-test point. v5's OptionalResolutionAction plumbing note and v7's global-vs-per-jar scope note are each real, verified value-adds beyond the human. v7 edges out v5 on the decisive greedy dimension and on citation precision (correct IT path vs v5's slightly misnamed test file).
